results.content:
chatgptricks Edited•

OpenAI just previewed GPT-5.6.  
  
The new model family includes three versions:  
  
- Sol for frontier-level work.  
- Terra for everyday tasks at lower cost.  
- Luna for fast, high-volume use.  
  
The biggest one is GPT-5.6 Sol, which OpenAI says is a major jump from GPT-5.5 and its strongest model yet for coding and cybersecurity.  
  
But access is limited for now.  
  
Only a small group of trusted partners can use it in Codex and the API, partly because of U.S. government concerns around powerful AI models.  
  
This is where AI is heading:  
More powerful models.  
More government oversight.  
More competition between OpenAI and Anthropic.  
  
What are your thoughts on this?  
  
Follow 👉@chatgptricks for more!

OpenAI just previewed GPT-5.6.

The new model family includes three versions:
Sol for frontier-level work.
Terra for everyday tasks at lower cost.
Luna for fast, high-volume use.

The biggest one is GPT-5.6 Sol, which OpenAI says is a major jump from GPT-5.5 and its strongest model yet for coding and cybersecurity.

But access is limited for now.

Only a small group of trusted partners can use it in Codex and the API, partly because of U.S. government concerns around powerful AI models.

results.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
results.content:
## GPT‑5.6 accelerates OpenAI

GPT‑5.6 is our strongest model yet for accelerating AI research. Inside OpenAI, researchers use it across the development loop: diagnosing failures, optimizing training systems, running experiments, and interpreting results. We already saw that acceleration and stronger adoption during the internal testing period of GPT‑5.6, as average daily output tokens per active researcher were more than twice the highest level observed for GPT‑5.5. [...] ## Efficient by default, maximum performance on demand

GPT‑5.6 Sol is our best coding model yet. On the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index, GPT‑5.6 Sol with max reasoning sets a new state of the art at 80, 2.8 points above Fable 5, while using less than half the output tokens, taking less than half the time, and costing about one-third less. That advantage extends across the family: Terra performs just above Fable 5, while Luna outperforms Opus 4.8; each does so in roughly one-third of the time, with about half as many output tokens, and at approximately one-quarter the estimated cost. It also sets new state-of-the-art results on Terminal‑Bench 2.1 and DeepSWE, which test complex command-line workflows and long-horizon engineering in real codebases.

results.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6 - TechCrunch
results.content:
OpenAI calls Sol its “best coding model yet,” and has explicitly compared it to Anthropic’s recently released (and much hyped) Fable.Using the Coding Agent Index, OpenAI claims that Sol “sets a new state of the art at 80, 2.8 points above Fable 5, while using less than half the output tokens, taking less than half the time, and costing about one-third less.”

It adds: “That advantage extends across the family: Terra performs just above Fable 5, while Luna outperforms Opus 4.8.”

The company says that 5.6 is now available across ChatGPT, Codex, and the OpenAI API. Availability per million tokens is priced as follows: Sol is $5 input / $30 output, Terra is $2.50 input / $15 output, and Luna is $1 input / $6 output.

Topics

AI, ChatGPT, gpt-5.6, OpenAI, sam altman [...] TechCrunch Brand Studio

Contact Us

Image Credits:Kevin Dietsch / Getty Images

AI

, Terra (a more intermediate option), and Luna (its budget-friendly option). These models expand what users can do across a variety of fields — with the company promising powerful capabilities in enterprise work, coding, and even scientific research.

CEO Sam Altman has promised that his company’s newest models are orders of magnitude more efficient and cost-effective than previous versions, recently telling CNBC that Sol is 54% more token efficient when it comes to AI coding tasks.

Most notably, the company calls 5.6 its “strongest cybersecurity model yet, achieving frontier performance with significantly fewer tokens.” [...] Seek %0-9

Live

00:00

26:18

26:18

However, GPT-5.6 and its attendant marketing seems most designed to take aim at OpenAI’s primary opponent, Anthropic. Anthropic has managed to make itself the likable underdog of the AI race, focusing fixedly on enterprise customers and winning a growing share of support as a result.

Not to be outdone, OpenAI cites theArtificial Analysis Coding Agent Index, a notable benchmarking metric, to claim that its latest family of models outshines Anthropic’s models at every turn.

GPT-5.6 (Generative Pre-trained Transformer 5.6) is a large language model (LLM) released by OpenAI on June 26, 2026. The initial release was limited; OpenAI later announced that GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna would launch publicly on July 9, 2026 at 10AM PDT, while preview access expanded globally.( It was released alongside GPT Live 1, formerly GPT-Bidi 1.(

GPT-5.6 comes in three versions, from least to most capable: Luna, Terra and Sol.(

results.title: GPT-5.6 IS HERE! BEST AI Model Ever? Beats Fable, Faster, & Cheaper! (Fully Tested)
results.content: sorts of storytelling, dialogues, audio, cinematography, and rendering all at once. That level of multimodal coherence is something we really haven't seen from any model yet. And this is just remarkable that GBT 5.6 Soul Ultra was able to output this. Overall, this is a really cool new model series from OpenAI and it's just really great to see that OpenAI is finally back and providing us one of the best models that we have seen in such a long time. It's definitely a great step forward and I am really excited for the next step that OpenAI will be taking with GBT6 which is likely going to be a really big and incredible model and I've already heard some pretty cool reports about it. Now I'll leave all the links that I used in today's video in the description below. I would essentially use [...] Open AI is officially back as today the company launched the entire GBT 5.6 family with Soul, Terra, and Luna now generally available across chat GBT codeex and the API following the earlier rollout restrictions. GBT 5.6 comes in three different tiers. Soul is built for long horizon coding and aentic work that requires planning, tool use, and reliable follow-through. Terra balances performance and cost for everyday professional workloads. Luna prioritizes speed and efficiency for well-defined high volume tasks. Across knowledge work evaluations, the GBT 5.6 Terra is reportedly surpassing GBT 5.5 while costing less. Whereas the GBT 5.6 Luna is nearly matching GPT 5.5's peak performance as well as being under half the estimated API costage. But the real headline is the GPT 5.6 Soul. OpenAI [...] costage. But the real headline is the GPT 5.6 Soul. OpenAI claims that Soul sets a new standard for intelligence across different domains as well as its efficiency in coding, knowledge work, cyber security, science, and agentic tasks while using fewer tokens and delivering results at lower cost. Performance-wise, the GBT 5.6 Soul is posting some seriously impressive numbers across agentic work, coding, computer use, cyber security, and research on agents/exam. PBC 5.6 6 Soul is reportedly reaching a 53.6 percentage, placing it 13.1 points ahead of Claude Fable 5 Adaptive. What makes the results even more impressive is the efficiency. At medium reasoning, it even still beats the Fable 5 with a substantial gain. And even Luna is reportedly outperforming Fable 5 adaptive at around 116th of
